assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Andrey, I am writing because your team had a payment stuck for 23 days on a contractor payout through Wise. That is exactly the kind of friction Mellow handles. Concrete boundary from line one: Mellow owns contractor documents, KYC, payment chain operations, and audit trail; legal qualification and strategic risk decisions remain on your side. Can I map out the exact flow in two lines?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.

criteria.short_reason: Language is adapted to the chosen persona.

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Andrey, I am writing because your team had a payment stuck for 23 days on a contractor payout through Wise. That is exactly the kind of friction Mellow handles. Concrete boundary from line one: Mellow owns contractor documents, KYC, payment chain operations, and audit trail; legal qualification and strategic risk decisions remain on your side. Can I map out the exact flow in two lines?

objections.objection: I see you did your homework. Immediate question: payment chain, documents, KYC — what's in your scope, what's not?
Fine. Send the flow and SLA.

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.evidence_quote: The flow: Mellow collects contractor documents and KYC upfront, owns the payment execution chain, and generates a full audit trail per payment with timestamps and named incident owner. Your 23-day hold becomes a logged, managed event with SLA — not a finance fire drill. We have seen teams go from 3-4 days of manual reconciliation per month to near zero. Can we do a focused 20-minute review this week?
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Andrey, I am writing because your team had a payment stuck for 23 days on a contractor payout through Wise. That is exactly the kind of friction Mellow handles. Concrete boundary from line one: Mellow owns contractor documents, KYC, payment chain operations, and audit trail; legal qualification and strategic risk decisions remain on your side. Can I map out the exact flow in two lines?
I see you did your homework. Immediate question: payment chain, documents, KYC — what's in your scope, what's not?

The flow: Mellow collects contractor documents and KYC upfront, owns the payment execution chain, and generates a full audit trail per payment with timestamps and named incident owner. Your 23-day hold becomes a logged, managed event with SLA — not a finance fire drill. We have seen teams go from 3-4 days of manual reconciliation per month to near zero. Can we do a focused 20-minute review this week?
Fine. Send the flow and SLA.
